events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON. Need analyze schedule. Let's parse image. Activities:
A Mobilize & layout [4d] pred -, starts day 0 ends 4.
B Underground rough-in [5d] pred A, starts 4 ends 9.
C Slab pour [4d] pred B, starts 9 ends 13.
CC Slab cure [3d] pred C, hatched lag, starts 13 ends 16.
D Framing [6d] pred CC, starts 16 ends 22.
E Roof dry-in [2d] pred D, starts 22 ends 24.
F Electrical rough [5d] pred E, starts 24 ends 29.
G Plumbing top-out [3d] pred E, starts 24 ends 27.
H HVAC rough [5d] pred E, starts 24 ends 29.
I Rough inspection [1d] pred F,G,H, starts 29 ends 30.
J Insulation [2d] pred I, starts 30 ends 32.
K Insulation inspection [1d] pred J, starts 32 ends 33.
L Drywall hang [4d] pred K, starts 33 ends 37.
M Tape & finish [4d] pred L, starts 37 ends 41.
N Paint [2d] pred M, starts 41 ends 43.
O Trim carpentry [3d] pred N, starts 43 ends 46.
P Flooring [1d] pred N, starts 43 ends 44.
Q Final inspection [1d] pred O,P, starts 46 ends 47.
Original project duration 47 workdays.
Disruption: Activity P (Flooring) needs 1 extra workday, duration becomes 2d. P starts after N finishes at 43, so P now ends 45. Q depends on O and P. O ends 46, P ends 45. Q starts 46 ends 47. No delay to project finish because O is critical path? Let's compute floats.
Critical path likely A-B-C-CC-D-E-F/H-I-J-K-L-M-N-O-Q = 47. P has float? P duration 1, N ends 43, Q starts 46. P must finish by 46 to not delay Q. P duration 2 => finishes 45, still before 46. So float absorbs. Project finish remains 47. Completion date does not slip. value = 0 workdays delay.
Need identify what drives answer: P is not on critical path; it has 2 days total float (P duration 1 plus slack before Q start 46? Actually N finishes 43, Q starts 46, window 3 days. P needs 2 days, leaving 1 day float). So 1-day extension absorbed by float.
Need output JSON with fields. decision? This is schedule impact assessment, not pass/fail of work quality. The task says decide what work can proceed, blocked, recovery plan. But required output shape includes decision pass/fail/needs_more_info. Since disruption absorbed, schedule remains valid; no dangerous/non-compliant. Could decision be "pass"? Maybe. risk low. work_stage in-progress. percent_complete estimate? The schedule shows all activities maybe not started? Stage in-progress actively in progress. We can estimate overall progress maybe around 0? But need numeric. Since scenario says in-progress but no specific percent. Could estimate based on schedule: maybe around 0 because lookahead schedule? But image shows full schedule bars, not actual progress. The stage is in-progress. Percent complete maybe 0? Hmm. The task: "percent_complete is your numeric estimate of overall progress." Without evidence of actual progress, maybe leave 0. But could estimate ~5%? Since only mobilize/layout maybe underway. However the image is a schedule, not site photo. We can set 0 or low. Let's set 0 because no actual progress evidence; schedule assessment only.
component_conditions empty list.
value 0.
sound_source etc irrelevant; use empty string or defaults? Schema says string. Use "" for sound_source, 0.0 event_time, 0.0 rate.
order empty list.
workable list of activity IDs that can still start? The task asks what work can proceed. Since P delayed but not blocking, all activities per schedule can proceed as planned. But maybe ask what work can proceed given disruption? P is delayed by material delivery; it cannot start until material arrives? The disruption note says needs 1 extra workday — material delivery slipped. It doesn't say P cannot start at all; duration extended. Workable activities unaffected. Could list all activities whose predecessors complete? At current time maybe early ones. But the task specifically asks "decide what work can proceed, what is blocked, and what recovery plan is valid". However required output fields include workable list of activity IDs when task asks what work can still start. It does ask in general. We need provide wo…
